leadText: Dazzling Gleam (Japanese: マジカルシャイン Magical Shine) is a damage-dealing Fairy-type move introduced in Generation VI. It is: TM99 from Generation VI to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon. TM32 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR92 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM21 in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM079 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM092 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation VIII 1.1.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.2 Generation IX 1.2.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Dazzling Gleam inflicts damage to all adjacent opponents. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Dazzling Gleam's power has been lowered to 75. It can only hit one target. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP S V The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash. LA The user emits a powerful flash of light to inflict damage on the target. ZA The user attacks with a powerful flash of light.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD MD RT DX It damages nearby enemies.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon the Series: Sun & Moon , Dazzling Gleam is consistently depicted as a move that makes contact .
